"The Women's Leadership and Resource Center and Campus Advocacy Network will be closed March 24-28, 2025.

If you experience any kind of gender-based or interpersonal violence (such as sexual assault, domestic/dating violence, harassment, or stalking) or need to talk to someone, you can contact a 24-hour hotline anytime to get support from an advocate via phone, text, or chat. We look forward to seeing you when we reopen on March 31.

Wishing you a safe spring break, Flames!

"Author, activist, and filmmaker Curtis Chin (@curtischin8) is coming to UIC! Join us for 2 events on this special day!

Tuesday, March 4, 2025
✒Memoir Writing Workshop: 12:30–1:45 PM | 215 Taft Hall & Zoom
📖 Book Reading & Signing: 3:00–4:30 PM | Daley Library 1-470 & Zoom

In his memoir, _Everything I Learned, I Learned in a Chinese Restaurant_, Chin shares his journey of growing up Asian in Detroit, coming out to his working-class immigrant family, and navigating the political and cultural landscape of the ‘80s.

Hear Curtis read from his book & grab a signed copy!
Free & open to the public | CART live captioning available

Check out the link in our bio to RSVP!

Hosted by the UIC AANAPISI Initiative and co-sponsored by @uicaasap, @uicgsc, @uicaarcc, @uiclibrary, @uicglas, @invisible2invincible, @restoriedbookshop, and @uicwlrc.

.

[ID: Curtis Chin, wearing a blue hooded sweatshirt and glasses, smiling. Below him is the cover of his book, Everything I Learned, I Learned in a Chinese Restaurant. Around them is text describing the book reading and writing workshop events.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICEvents #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #CurtisChin #AsianAmericanVoices".

caption:
"Black women have long championed self- and community care for survival, resistance, and healing. As Audre Lorde reminds us, self-care "is not self-indulgence—it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare."

This Black History Month, we honor the legacy of women who have redefined care, community, and activism. From rest as resistance to storytelling as liberation, their contributions continue to shape the fight for justice.

How do you practice radical self-care and community care? Drop a comment below!

.

[ID 1: An orange background with a torn-paper collage. Bold black text features an Audre Lorde quote: "Taking care of myself is not self-indulgence, it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare."

ID 2: A black-and-white portrait of @taranajaneen with a purple gradient behind her. Text reads:"Tarana Burke
Founder of the metoomvmt.
Advocates for healing as a form of justice, centering survivors' well-being in activism. Encourages storytelling and community support as self-care."

ID 3:  A black-and-white photo of Audre Lorde speaking into a microphone. Text reads:
"Audre Lorde
Poet, Activist, and Feminist
Taught that self-care is a political act, especially for marginalized people. Practiced self-care through writing, reflection, and community-building."

ID 4: A photo of a silhouette holding a megaphone near a window. Text reads:
"Mariame Kaba
Prison Abolitionist & Organizer
Emphasizes collective care and mutual aid as essential for justice. Promotes restorative justice as an alternative to harm and punishment."

ID 5: A portrait of Tricia Hersey sitting on a couch. Text reads:
Tricia Hersey
Founder of @TheNapMinistry
Advocates for rest as resistance against grind culture. Believes in reclaiming rest, slowing down, and dreaming as radical self-care.

ID 6: A photo of Ericka Huggins speaking into a microphone. Text reads:
"Ericka Huggins
Black Panther & Educator
Introduced meditation and mindfulness in activism. Used spiritual wellness as a tool for resilience and community empowerment."]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #BlackHistoryMonth #BHM #CommunityCare #RadicalSelfCare #UICBHM".

🧵Tatreez, bordado, embroidery: Stitching connections between Palestine, Mexico and Central America🧵

This program is presented by the @uiclcc and the @arabamcc 

When: Monday, February 17th, 2025
Where: Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2
Time: 5:00pm-7:00pm

❗️❗️RSVP REQUIRED: Registration closed❗️❗️

Join us for a brief presentation and hands-on workshop learning the historical significance of the traditional Palestinian embroidery practice, known as tatreez. caption:
"Before we close out January, we want to shine a light on National Stalking Awareness Month.

Stalking is about control, not the victim’s behavior. Victims shouldn’t have to shrink their lives to stay safe. Instead, let’s hold stalkers accountable and create supportive communities.

Learn the warning signs:
👉 Surveillance
👉 Life invasion
👉 Intimidation
👉 Interference

Ask yourself, how would you help a friend? Supporting victims means listening, believing them, and prioritizing their safety.

Visit the Link in our Bio for more resources, facts, and ways to take action from SPARC (@followuslegally).

.

[ID 1: Text on a navy blue background reads, "IT’S STALKING" and "Surveillance, Life invasion, Intimidation, Interference through sabotage or attack." Icons representing stalking activities, such as cameras, locks, and location pins, line the bottom. The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is at the bottom.

ID 2: A person wearing a hospital gown on a dark blue background featuring medical symbols such as stethoscopes, syringes, and hospital beds in lighter blue. Text reads, "Stalking is a PUBLIC HEALTH ISSUE." The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is displayed below. 

ID 3: A figure stands at a crossroads with directional signs pointing to Home, Work, Grocery Store, Gym, and School. Large text at the top reads, "For many stalking victims, nowhere feels safe." The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is at the bottom.

ID 4: A person is hunched over, carrying the burden of several signs of phrases often told to stalking victims: "You should really get a new phone number," "Don't go out alone," and "Get off social media." On the right, white text reads, "Stalking victims are often advised to make their lives smaller. Help victims balance safety and freedom." The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is at the bottom.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #Stalking #StalkingAwareness #StalkingAwarenessMonth #NationalStalkingAwarenessMonth".

caption:
"Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. taught us that greatness is rooted in service:

"Everybody can be great...because anybody can serve. You don’t have to have a college degree to serve. You don’t have to make your subject and verb agree to serve. You only need a heart full of grace, a soul generated by love."

As we reflect on his legacy, let’s remember that change starts with each of us. Whether it’s lending a hand, speaking up for justice, or showing compassion, every effort counts. Together, we can build stronger, kinder communities.

caption:
"Help us support the Undergraduate Student Government’s (@uicusg)  Food Drive in collaboration with the Wellness Center! 

WLRC is proud to host a donation box for this initiative through Tuesday, November 26. All donations will go directly to the UIC Pop-Up Pantry, which will help provide essential resources for students in need.

Drop-off Location: 1700 SSB
Through Tuesday, November 26

We encourage you to donate non-perishable items like canned goods, cereals, nut butter, and more! Together, we can make a difference and ensure our UIC community feels supported!

.

caption:
"November 20th marks Transgender Day of Remembrance, and we’re honoring and uplifting the voices of our transgender community here at UIC and beyond. TDOR is a time to remember those lost to anti-trans violence and to recommit ourselves to creating safer, more inclusive spaces. 

Check out the Gender and Sexuality Center (@uicgsc) and UIC Law Pro Bono Litigation Clinic (@uic_law) event today. Visit the link in our bio for all the details!

Name & Gender Marker Change Information Session
Wednesday, November 20 2024
12:00–1:30 PM CST
Virtual on Zoom 
Learn how to legally change your name and correct your gender marker in Illinois with guidance from UIC Law’s Pro Bono Litigation Clinic and Gender & Sexuality Center.

.

[ID 1: A soft gradient background with a focus on three lit candles. Text reads, “Remembering Transgender Lives with Love” and “Today is Transgender Day of Remembrance” in bold white letters. At the bottom are the logos for UIC Women’s Leadership and Resource Center and Campus Advocacy Network.

ID 2: A background featuring a transgender pride flag waving softly. The text reads, “Transgender Day of Remembrance (TDOR),” followed by an explanation: “What is it? Transgender Day of Remembrance is an annual observance on November 20th to honor the memory of transgender people whose lives were lost due to anti-transgender violence. Why It Matters: - Raises awareness of violence faced by transgender individuals. - Encourages solidarity and advocacy for trans rights.”

ID 3: A colorful design with a blue and pink background. The text reads, “Name & Gender Marker Change Information Session” followed by the event date, time, and RSVP link. The description below reads, “In recognition of Transgender Day of Remembrance, the Pro Bono Litigation Clinic at UIC Law will go over the process for legally changing your name in Illinois and correcting your gender marker on identification documents.” At the bottom are the logos for the UIC Law Pro Bono Litigation Clinic and Gender and Sexuality Center.]

.

#uiccan #uicwlrc #uiclife #uicevents #uicbound #uicflames #transdayofremembrance #transrightsarehumanrights".
